title: AIIR — AI Incident Record v0.1
description: Vendor-neutral, tamper-evident, replayable record of a single AI incident on a fielded military AI system. DRAFT v0.1, awaiting ratification. Incident = policy gate HALT_DEFER_TO_HUMAN and/or any BREACH-severity violation. Maps to draft 10 U.S.C. 2224b ('covered AI incidents', H.R. 8800 Sec. 1501 — committee print, not enacted law). Plain JSON; emittable with the Python stdlib alone. Tamper-EVIDENT, not tamper-proof.

aiir_schema_version.description: Schema identity pin (literal-string convention shared with Crucible packet schema_version).

record_id.description: Globally unique record id: 'aiir-' + UUIDv4 (stdlib uuid).

incident_class.description: Statutory prong mapping per draft 2224b: outside approved guardrails | materially degraded performance | rule unevaluable, failed closed (reportable per fail-closed doctrine; founder decision pending on keeping this distinct).

vendor.description: Emitting vendor identifier. Vendor-neutral: any vendor can populate.

model_fingerprint.description: Optional model/weights fingerprint or attestation reference (referee: model_fingerprint).

deployment_context.description: Optional deployment/site label. No proprietary telemetry required.

obs_id.description: Observation id; joins this record to chain leaves (referee: obs_id).

ts_emitted_utc.description: When the vendor system emitted the decision.

ts_observed_utc.description: When the monitor observed it (latency = observed - emitted).

decision_type.description: Vendor decision type, e.g. detection | track | recommendation | llm_summary | malformed. Open string: vendor-neutral.

confidence.description: Vendor-reported confidence; null if not provided (null is itself evaluable under fail-closed rules).

ddil_profile.description: Comms/degradation profile in effect at observation time (e.g. nominal, satellite_link).

upstream_provenance.description: Opaque provenance references for inputs (sensor frames, upstream model outputs). May be empty — absence is policy-evaluable.

payload_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the exact observed raw bytes. Raw payload is NOT in this record (classification hygiene); it lives in the referenced chain leaf for replay.

policy_id.description: Identifier of the authorized-parameters policy in force (the 'approved guardrails').

policy_sha256.description: SHA-256 of the exact policy document evaluated — pins which guardrails were 'approved' at incident time.

clause_id.description: Policy clause violated (referee field name: rule), e.g. geofence, confidence_floor, latency_sla, classification_ceiling, ddil_profile, forbidden_class_adjacency, forbidden_decision_type, provenance_missing, stale_decision, malformed, unevaluable_rule.

detail.description: Human-readable detail; display-only, carries no machine semantics.

taxonomy_tags.description: Machine taxonomy tags (e.g. authorized-parameters, spatial, fail-closed).

gate_outcome.description: Policy gate result (referee: PolicyDecision.gate). Advisory-only: the gate signals; it never commands the vendor system. An incident record normally carries HALT_DEFER_TO_HUMAN; PASS appears only when BREACH-severity violations were logged without a halt.

chain_head.description: Head hash of the tamper-evident chain at record-emit time. Consumers verify offline by replaying the chain file (reference impl: referee chain.verify_dir, ~40 lines stdlib).

merkle_root.description: Merkle root over all leaf hashes at record-emit time.

leaf_index.description: Index of the violation leaf this record corresponds to.

rfc3161_timestamp.description: Optional RFC 3161 timestamp token (base64) anchoring the chain head to an external time authority; null when not yet anchored.

tamper_evident_not_tamper_proof.description: Honesty marker carried in-band, verbatim from the reference bundle format.

status.description: Result of the most recent offline chain verification. Whitelist only — AIIR never asserts 'COMPLIANT' or 'CERTIFIED'; integrity of the record is the only claim.

first_bad_leaf.description: Index of first failing leaf when status=FAIL; null otherwise.

verifier.description: Identifier/version of the verification tool used.

level.description: Record-level classification (order per referee CLASSIFICATION_ORDER). The record carries hashes, not payloads, so it may ride below the source-data marking.

ext.description: Extension namespace. Vendor- or program-specific fields go here, never in core. Keys MUST be namespaced '<ns>/<key>' (e.g. 'vendor.example/track_quality'). Values are unconstrained JSON.

utc_timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp, 'Z' suffix mandatory (machine-checkable; stdlib datetime emittable).
